chapter_context.summary: David declares Ornan's threshing floor as the site for the Temple, gathers vast building materials, and gives a solemn charge to Solomon and Israel's leaders to build the House.

outline.text: Ornan's Site Declared Temple Ground; Materials Prepared
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ornan's Site Declared Temple Ground; Materials Prepared (22:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David's Charge to Solomon Concerning Building the Temple
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Charge to Solomon Concerning Building the Temple (22:6-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David Commands the Princes of Israel to Help Solomon
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Commands the Princes of Israel to Help Solomon (22:17-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 22, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
